response.justification: Bogdan brings a genuinely relevant profile: a current Staff-level role building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq aligns directly with the AI-native engineering environment this role demands, and his time at Sociomantic demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems experience at scale. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from the resume and skills inventory, which is a hard gap at Staff level. Stakeholder management, incident response, and on-call ownership are also unaddressed. The distributed systems work at meaningful scale is also six-plus years old. Worth a screening call to surface Python proficiency and operational readiness before advancing.
strengths: Active hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq — directly matches the AI-native engineering baseline the role treats as table stakes
Proven high-throughput distributed systems background at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O and micro-services
Consistent Staff-level titles across multiple employers, polyglot background, and strong TypeScript depth across recent roles
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list — the role lists it as a must-have technical skill, making this a blocking gap that must be resolved before advancing
High-throughput distributed systems work at real scale is dated (2018–2020); current role leans toward tooling and agentic workflows rather than systems operating under reliability SLOs
No evidence of stakeholder management across functions, incident-response ownership, or participation in an on-call rotation — all explicitly required non-technical skills
